description_long: This bungalow features a low, wide profile with a prominent, dark brown cross-gabled roof and brick exterior walls. The front facade is defined by a covered porch supported by wooden beams, with stonework accents at the base of the walls. Windows are symmetrically arranged on the front and rear, with grouped panes and dark trim, while the sides have smaller, paired windows and additional stonework. The roof extends outward to provide deep eaves, typical of Craftsman-style architecture, and a single brick chimney is visible. The structure sits close to the ground, emphasizing its horizontal lines and inviting entryway. The overall appearance is solid, with a mix of brick, stone, and wood materials contributing to a warm, residential feel.
description: A low, wide brick bungalow with a cross-gabled dark roof, covered front porch, and symmetrical window arrangement. Stone and wood accents highlight its Craftsman-style design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, main entrance, and symmetrical windows with stonework at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the deep eaves, paired side windows, and stonework on the lower wall.
description_view_2: Rear view with a wide roof overhang, grouped windows, and a visible chimney.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing similar features to view 1, with paired windows and stone accents.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with paired side windows
